title: Foreign Pollution Fee Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill creates a new law that charges extra fees on products brought into the United States from other countries. The fees are based on how much pollution was created when making those products. The bill says that American companies have to follow stricter environmental rules than companies in other countries like China, which makes it harder for American businesses to compete. By charging these pollution fees on foreign products, the law tries to make the competition more fair for American manufacturers who spend more money to keep the environment clean.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This Act may be called the Foreign Pollution Fee Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Sense of Congress; Purpose
sections.text: Congress believes that the United States has led the world in reducing carbon emissions over the past fifteen years, cutting more emissions than any other nation. The United States economy is fifty five percent more carbon efficient than the global average. On average, goods made in China create more than three times the carbon emissions of the same goods made in America. Russian made goods produce five times the emissions. This gives foreign polluters an unfair cost advantage over American manufacturers. Federal environmental regulations cost the United States economy an estimated four hundred billion dollars each year. This places a heavy burden on American businesses and workers. Manufacturers in the United States face huge environmental regulatory compliance costs, averaging seventeen thousand two hundred dollars per employee. These are costs that foreign competitors, especially in China, do not have to pay. American businesses spend a higher percentage of their money on environmental compliance than many of their global competitors. This makes it harder to compete internationally. Because of these costs, companies in the United States have lost market share to foreign producers operating under weak, poorly enforced, or nonexistent environmental standards. China is by far the world's worst air and water polluter, responsible for thirty percent of global carbon emissions. The Chinese Communist Party effectively pays for its exports by refusing to enforce basic environmental protections. This undercuts responsible manufacturers in the United States. China's state controlled industries operate as an extension of the Communist Party, using harmful trade practices including environmental exploitation to eliminate American competition and expand Beijing's control over global markets. United States trade policy has given foreign polluters a competitive edge at the expense of American workers for decades, rewarding bad actors while punishing responsible manufacturers in the United States. China has been the main beneficiary of these policies. The United States has lost approximately five million jobs in the last two decades, with half of that loss directly caused by the growing trade deficit with China. Recognizing and rewarding manufacturers in the United States for their environmental leadership would strengthen domestic industry, create high paying jobs, and reduce America's dependence on high emitting producers like China and Russia. The purpose of this Act is to level the playing field for American workers and manufacturers by ensuring that China and other foreign adversaries cannot exploit weak environmental standards, lack of enforcement, and noncompliance to gain an unfair advantage in global trade.

sections.title: Section 3  Rule of Construction
sections.text: Nothing in this Act or any changes made by this Act shall be understood to authorize the creation of any carbon tax, fee, pricing, or other system that adds costs to any covered product that is made in the United States and sold, used, further refined, or distributed within the United States or exported to another country for sale or use.
